content: The 2024-2025 SOTA advances in active learning agent memory can be repurposed to solve a core flaw of curiosity-driven intrinsic reward agents: the noisy 'curiosity trap' of prioritizing uninformative novel stimuli over high-value long-horizon knowledge. By framing active learning's memory pruning and prioritization framework to filter curiosity-driven exploration, agents can retain only high-impact unexpected experiences that push long-term task competence, rather than wasting capacity on trivial novelty.
